2604.24219.title: Adaptive ToR: Complexity-Aware Tree-Based Retrieval for Pareto-Optimal Multi-Intent NLU
2604.24219.paper_summary: Multi-intent natural language understanding requires retrieval systems that simultaneously achieve high accuracy and computational efficiency, yet existing approaches apply either uniform single-step retrieval that compromises recall or fixed-depth hierarchical decomposition that introduces excessive latency regardless of query complexity. This paper proposes Adaptive Tree-of-Retrieval (Adaptive ToR), a complexity-aware retrieval architecture that dynamically configures retrieval topology based on query characteristics. The system integrates four components: (1) a Query Tree Classifier computing a Query Complexity Index from weighted linguistic signals to route queries to either a rapid single-step path or an adaptive-depth hierarchical path; (2) a Tree-Based Retrieval module that recursively decomposes complex queries into focused sub-queries calibrated to predicted complexity; (3) an Adaptive Pruning Module employing two-stage filtering combining quantitative similarity gating with semantic relevance evaluation to suppress exponential node growth; and (4) a Retrieval Reranking Layer featuring a deduplicator-first pipeline and global LLM rescoring for production efficiency. Evaluation on the NLU++ benchmark (2,693 multi-intent queries across Banking and Hotel domains) yields 29.07% Subset Accuracy and 71.79% Micro-F1, a 9.7% relative improvement over fixed-depth baselines, while reducing latency by 37.6%, LLM invocations by 43.0%, and token consumption by 9.8%. Depth-wise analysis reveals that 26.92% of queries resolve within three seconds (2.45s mean latency) via single-step routing (d=0: 37.9% Subset Accuracy, 74.8% Micro-F1), while token consumption scales by 4.9x across depths, validating complexity-aware resource allocation and establishing Pareto-optimal balance across accuracy, latency, and computational efficiency.
2604.24219.paper_summary_zh: 多意圖自然語言理解需要同時達到高準確率和計算效率的檢索系統，但現有的方法要麼應用統一的單步檢索，這會影響召回率，要麼使用固定深度的層次分解，這會引入過多的延遲，無論查詢的複雜性如何。本文提出了自適應檢索樹（Adaptive Tree-of-Retrieval, Adaptive ToR），這是一種基於查詢特徵動態配置檢索拓撲的複雜性感知檢索架構。該系統整合了四個組件：（1）查詢樹分類器，從加權語言信號計算查詢複雜性指數，以將查詢路由到快速的單步路徑或自適應深度的層次路徑；（2）基於樹的檢索模塊，將複雜查詢遞歸分解為針對預測複雜性調整的聚焦子查詢；（3）自適應修剪模塊，採用兩階段過濾，將定量相似性閘與語義相關性評估相結合，以抑制指數級的節點增長；以及（4）檢索重排序層，具有去重器優先的管道和全球LLM重評分以提高生產效率。在NLU++基準（涵蓋銀行和酒店領域的2,693個多意圖查詢）上的評估結果顯示，子集準確率為29.07%，微F1為71.79%，相對於固定深度基線提高了9.7%，同時將延遲減少了37.6%，LLM調用減少了43.0%，令牌消耗減少了9.8%。深度分析顯示，26.92%的查詢在三秒內解決（平均延遲2.45秒），通過單步路由（d=0: 37.9%子集準確率，74.8%微F1），而令牌消耗在不同深度之間擴展了4.9倍，驗證了基於複雜性的資源分配，並在準確性、延遲和計算效率之間建立了帕累托最優平衡。
2604.24219.author: Hee-Kyong Yoo et.al.
2604.24219.authors: Hee-Kyong Yoo, Wonbae Kim, Hyocheol Ahn
